evals.prompt: There's a Dockerfile in this repo — can you check it actually builds and that the container starts and responds on port 8080? I don't have docker desktop on this machine btw
evals.expected_output: Uses podman machine preflight, builds with `podman build` (Dockerfile unchanged), runs with `podman run -p 8080:...`, verifies the endpoint responds, and does NOT suggest installing Docker or rewriting the Dockerfile

evals.prompt: spin up the docker-compose.yml dev stack (postgres + redis + the api) and confirm the api container can reach redis
evals.expected_output: Runs `podman compose up -d` or `docker-compose up -d` against the compose file unchanged, checks service health/connectivity (e.g., exec into api container and ping redis by service name), tears down cleanly

evals.prompt: our github actions workflow does 'docker build -t app .' then 'docker run app npm test' — reproduce that locally so I can debug the failing test without pushing commits
evals.expected_output: Runs the same docker commands verbatim through the compat layer (podman verbs or alias/shim), does not translate the CI YAML to podman syntax, and notes rootful-vs-CI-runner differences if relevant to the failure
